title: Data ethics and reporting
summary: Treat privacy, fairness, transparency, and communication quality as part of the analysis itself rather than as polish added at the end.

keywords: Ethics Throughout the Data Science Cycle

items.prompt: Why should ethics be considered across the whole data science cycle instead of only at deployment?

solution.summary: Bias, privacy loss, and misuse can enter during collection, cleaning, modeling, visualization, and reporting, so waiting until the end is too late.

hints: Trust grows when caveats are visible.
items.prompt: Which reporting habit most improves trust?

choices.label: State assumptions, limitations, and uncertainty clearly.
Hide weak results to keep the story cleaner.
Report only one metric with no context.

solution.summary: Transparent reporting includes assumptions, uncertainty, limitations, and provenance so others can judge the work responsibly.

items.prompt: Name one fairness question you should ask before deploying a model.

solution.summary: A useful fairness check asks whether errors or benefits are distributed unevenly across meaningful groups.

items.prompt: Imagine you must brief a non-technical stakeholder on an analysis. What belongs in a strong executive summary?

solution.summary: A strong summary is brief, decision-oriented, and honest about limitations.
checklist: The main finding and the action it supports.
The uncertainty or risk around that finding.
What data and methods shaped the result.
